title: HUMAN CAPITAL AND INCOME LEVEL IN ANDEAN FAMILY FARMING FOR SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT: EMPIRICAL EVIDENCE IN A RURAL PARISH OF SAN LUIS, ECUADOR
author: Carla Sofía Arguello Guadalupe, Mike Joffre Coral Erazo, Jorge Daniel Cordova Lliquin, José Abelardo Paucar Camacho, Adriana Catalina Guzmán Guaraca, Iván Alfredo Ríos García, Andrea Patricia Guapi Auquilla, Adrián Dios-Vicente
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The   aim   of   this   thesis   is   to   study   the   socio-economic   characterization   of small   farmers   in   rural   areas   through a case study for the parish of San Luis, in the province of Chimborazo (Ecuador). The theoretical framework used   allowed   us   to   establish   the   importance   of   this   activity   in   the   economic,   social,   cultural,   technological   and  institutional spheres of Ecuador and Latin America, and then to concentrate on the particularities of the parish  of San Luis. The methodology used is characterized by a mixed, qualitative and quantitative method: firstly,  through     a     bibliographical review,     the     geographical area of interest     was analyzed     and     related to the theoretical  framework linked to family farming; Secondly, data was collected through a survey to 254 farmers in the parish. The results made it possible to analyze 67 qualitative and quantitative variables, which offer highly  relevant data on the     productive,     social and economic characteristics     of small farmers in the parish. In     addition,  an   estimate   was   made   of   the   income,   production   costs   and   rent   or   gross   margin   for   the   four   main   crops   in   the parish of San Luis.

keywords: Family Farming, Small-Scale Farmers, Case Study, Ecuador, Chimborazo.
